rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL J. MOATS ET AL VS. MICHAEL TSANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Determining Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 216, 2026, line 10. DEFENDANTS NRT WEST, INC., NAOMI GLASS' Amended Motion For Order Determining Good Faith Settlement. Defendants NRT West, Inc. dba Coldwell Banker Global Luxury ("NRT West") and Defendant Naomi Glass move for an order determining that their settlement with plaintiffs is in good faith pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, section 877.6. No objections were made. For good cause shown, the court finds the settlement to be in good faith within the meaning of that statute. The court will sign the proposed order that was submitted by the moving party on January 14, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: LINDA CAMERENA VS. JEWISH COMMUNITY CENTER OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 26, 2026, line 2. DEFENDANT JEWISH COMMUNITY CENTER OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant Jewish Community Center's motion to strike portions of plaintiff Linda Camarena's First Amended Complaint is denied. A motion to strike lies to remove irrelevant, false, or improper matter, or parts of a pleading not in conformity with law, court rules, or court orders. (Code Civ. Proc. 435.) Camarena's pleading about JCC's alleged violation of various laws in operating its facilities is not irrelevant, false, or improper. JCC is correct that negligence per se is not a separate cause of action, but there is nothing improper about alleging a violation of law in a complaint for negligence. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: CEDAR MCREE VS. OSCAR JAHIR HERNANDEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 26, 2026, line 9. DEFENDANTS OSCAR HERNANDEZ, JOSE HERNANDEZ, MARIA DOMINGUEZ'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT. The court strikes plaintiff's late-filed opposition and orders plaintiff's counsel to meet and confer with defendant pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, section 430.41. The court continues the demurrer for hearing to February 26, 2026. If the parties are unable to resolve the issues raised by the demurrer after meeting and conferring, plaintiff may file a timely opposition based on the new hearing date. Plaintiff shall also include a declaration confirming that informal resolution efforts occurred pursuant to the Code. Defendants may file a timely reply. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint (Ccp 425.16)
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 26, 2026, line 11. DEFENDANTS TERENCE REDMOND, MELISSA REDMOND'S Special Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint (CCP 425.16). Per separate written order, Judge Van Aken is recused. The motion is continued to February 10, 2026, to be heard by in department 301 before Judge Mayfield at 1:30 p.m. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: CLAUDE PERASSO ET AL VS. TERENCE REDMOND ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder To Defendants Coombs & Dunlap Llps And David Dells Special Motion To Strike Complaint Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 425.16

The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely.
